title: Rustic Bookcase from Fence Slats
summary: A 1x2 frame carries the load; weathered cedar fence pickets skin the outside for a reclaimed look without hunting down actual reclaimed wood.
description:
The honest structure here is a 1x2 box — two ladder-like sides joined front to back, with notched shelf supports let into the sides at even intervals. Cedar fence pickets are cladding, not structure: they cover the back, trim the front edges, and finish the top, giving the whole thing a weathered, reclaimed look while the actual load-bearing frame is straight, square dimensional lumber underneath.

That split matters for sequencing. Build and square the 1x2 carcass completely first, then measure your own bookcase for the picket pieces rather than trusting the source dimensions — fence pickets vary board to board, and the plan tells you outright to wait and cut them to fit as you go. Notch the shelf supports so their flat face is up; that's what you nail the shelves to, and it's easy to install one upside down if you're not paying attention.

tools.note: For ripping extra width off the notched shelf supports and shaving picket pieces to fit.
Fence pickets are rough-sawn; expect to sand more than you would on surfaced lumber.

materials.name: Pine, 1x2, 8 ft
materials.note: The structural sides, top, and shelf supports.

materials.name: Cedar fence picket
materials.note: Back, front trim, and shelf edging. Buy an extra one or two — boards vary and you're cutting to fit as you go.

cutList.note: Two per side; these form the vertical ladder frame.
Join the two side ladders into one carcass.

steps.title: Build the two side ladders
steps.body: Using glue and 1-1/2" nails, build each side as a rectangle from two 60" rails and two 9-1/2" rungs. Check for square before the glue sets — these are the two long faces of the bookcase, and any twist here shows up in every shelf.

steps.title: Join the sides into a carcass
steps.body: Connect the two side ladders with four 13-1/2" cross braces at the front and back, using glue and nails. This gives you a full 3D box frame — the skeleton the shelves and cladding attach to.

steps.title: Lay out and install shelf supports
steps.body: Measure and mark both sides at 15", 30", and 45" up from the bottom. Glue and nail 9-1/2" 1x2 pieces at each mark, flat side facing up — that flat face is what the shelf boards land on, so don't install one rotated the wrong way.

steps.title: Cut and attach the top
steps.body: Measure the actual width of your assembled bookcase — don't trust a plan dimension over your own carcass — and cut two fence picket pieces to fit. Attach with glue and nails.

steps.title: Cut and attach the back
steps.body: Measure the height of the back opening and cut three picket slats to fit. Expect a little overlap where they meet the sides — fit is more important than an exact source measurement, since picket width varies board to board.

steps.title: Cut, notch, and fit the shelves
steps.body: Measure your actual shelf openings — the plan's builder got 16-1/2", yours may differ — and cut eight picket pieces to that length. Trace a scrap 1x2's outline onto each end and notch it out so the shelf drops down over the supports flush. Glue and nail the shelves to the supports, then run a pass on the table saw to trim any un-notched edge that's fighting the fit.

steps.title: Trim the front edges
steps.body: Measure your front opening width — expect around 2-1/8" per side, but confirm on your own carcass — and rip two trim pieces from one picket board on the table saw; the leftover 1" strip becomes the inner shelf trim. Attach the side trim with glue and nails, then the middle shelf trim using the thinner ripped scrap, saving the full-width trim for the top and bottom.

steps.title: Sand and finish
steps.body: Sand every rough-sawn face and edge — fence pickets come off the mill rougher than surfaced lumber, so budget real time here. Ease any sharp picket edges before this piece goes anywhere near a bedroom.

images.alt: Rustic bookcase clad in weathered cedar fence pickets over a square pine frame
